problem_addressed: Open-set active learning where models must handle both known and unknown classes while maintaining high query precision from early training stages.
core_claim: Their method achieves superior control and stability in purity regulation, maintaining high query precision from early training stages through a novel purity metric and flexible sampling strategy.
key_contributions: Purity-based candidate selection using auxiliary head outputs
Precision-based candidate refinement with calibrated target query precision
Information-based final query selection using Jensen-Shannon divergence
novelty: Introduces no additional hyperparameters while achieving stable, high-precision queries from the outset, unlike prior methods that show significant fluctuations and limited adaptive sampling.
method_summary: Trains classifier with LCE for primary head and LEDL for auxiliary head, computes purity margins using auxiliary outputs, fits GMM on logit margins to model purity regimes, and performs multi-stage selection (purity → precision → information) to select queries.
method_components: Purity margin computation using auxiliary head outputs
GMM fitting on logit margins to model purity regimes
key_innovation: The purity metric Spurity(x) = maxc∈Ck oc - maxc∈Cˆu oc which effectively distinguishes between known and unknown classes, enabling precise candidate selection and stable purity regulation.
assumptions: The auxiliary head can provide meaningful outputs for both known and unknown classes
Purity regimes can be effectively modeled by a 3-component GMM
High-purity samples provide greater utility when the model is undertrained
theoretical_grounding: References prior work [21] about high-purity samples providing greater utility when undertrained, but no explicit proofs or bounds are provided in the extracted content.
title: Revisiting Unknowns: Towards Effective and Efficient Open-Set Active Learning
